# Question Tree: Bid Strategy

Stress-test the bid/no-bid decision and gate readiness. Work through every
major assumption that could flip the recommendation.

---

### 1. Opportunity fit

"Is this opportunity squarely in our sweet spot — or are we stretching to
reach minimum competitive posture?"

_Recommended answer_: Name the 2–3 capabilities you'd stake the bid on.
If you can't name them immediately, that's the first risk.

---

### 2. PWin estimate

"What's our current PWin estimate, and what are the top 3 assumptions it
rests on?"

_Recommended answer_: PWin above 40% usually justifies B&P investment.
Probe whether it's based on evidence (customer engagement, incumbent signals)
or hope.

---

### 3. Incumbent assessment

"Who's the incumbent? How well are they performing? What's their biggest
vulnerability on this recompete?"

_Recommended answer_: Every recompete has at least one incumbent
vulnerability — performance gap, rate creep, key personnel attrition, or
scope mismatch. If you can't name one, the intelligence work isn't done.

---

### 4. Customer relationship

"What's our actual relationship with the CO, the PM, and the TPOC? Who on
our team has met them in person, and when?"

_Recommended answer_: Submitting a capability statement is not a
relationship. Push for named contacts with recent dated interactions.

---

### 5. Must-win or nice-to-win?

"Is this a must-win for strategic or financial reasons — or are we bidding
because it appeared on a pipeline report?"

_Recommended answer_: Must-wins get aggressive B&P, partner investments, and
executive attention. Nice-to-wins get templates and hope.

---

### 6. Teaming

"Do we have the full solution, or do we need partners? Who are the top two
teaming candidates, and why haven't we locked them up yet?"

_Recommended answer_: Teaming decisions made after RFP release are almost
always too late. If we're still searching, that's a PWin signal.

---

### 7. Discriminators

"What's our single biggest discriminator — the thing only we can truthfully
claim for THIS opportunity?"

_Recommended answer_: If the best answer is "our past performance" or "our
team quality," we don't have a real discriminator yet.

---

### 8. B&P investment

"How much B&P are we committing, and is it proportional to the TCV and our
PWin estimate?"

_Recommended answer_: B&P should scale with TCV and PWin. Underfunding a
must-win is one of the most common and costly capture mistakes.

---

### 9. Risk factors

"What's the single risk that could make us regret bidding this, and what
would have to be true for that risk to materialize?"

_Recommended answer_: OCI exposure, key person dependency, aggressive
schedule, and scope ambiguity are the most common late-stage killers.

---

### 10. Decision

"If you had to commit the B&P budget right now — bid or no-bid? What single
piece of information would flip that answer?"

_Recommended answer_: Force a crisp recommendation. Fence-sitting at gate
review is a resource allocation failure.
